pattern: For every item, the only partners that make a power-of-two sum are the (few) power-of-two values minus that item's own value -- so a running frequency map lets you count all valid earlier partners for each item in one left-to-right pass instead of checking every pair.
hints: A value only has a bounded number of possible 'power of two minus itself' complements, since values are capped and there are at most ~22 relevant powers of two to consider.

Walk the array once keeping a frequency count of values seen so far. For each new value v, for every power of two p >= v (and p reachable given the value cap), add the current count of (p - v) to your running answer, then increment the count of v.
Take the final answer modulo 1e9+7, and remember two different indices with the same deliciousness value still form distinct good meals -- the frequency counts (not a set) capture that automatically.

edge_inputs.description: statement example one, distinct odd values
statement example two, many repeated values
two zeros, 0+0=0 which is not a power of two so no good meal
two identical powers of two whose sum doubles to the next power of two
no pair sums to a power of two
value at the maximum cap paired with itself

stress.description: random deliciousness values with many collisions to stress the modulo counting
